Webathon 2026, SNS College of Technology, Vibe Coding Web Design Challenge, Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, 13th March 2026

About Event

WEBATHON is a creative Vibe Coding Web Design Challenge conducted under TEXPERIA 2026 by the Department of AIML & IT at SNS College of Technology, Coimbatore. This event invites participants to design and develop an innovative website using chatbot-assisted coding based on an on-spot theme.

The competition focuses on creative UI design, user experience, and smart use of AI tools, providing students an exciting platform to showcase their web development and design skills.

WEBATHON – On-Spot Web Development Hackathon

Event Guests

Live Folk Musical Performance by Anthony Daasan (Folk Marley).

Pro Nites

Get ready for an electrifying night of music at TEXPERIA 2026 featuring a special Live Folk Musical Performance by Anthony Daasan (Folk Marley). Experience the vibrant energy of Tamil folk music as Anthony Daasan brings the stage alive with his powerful voice, rhythmic beats, and engaging performance. Join us for an unforgettable evening of music, culture, and celebration.

Agentic AI Ideathon – TEXPERIA 2026

Agentic AI Ideathon – TEXPERIA 2026, SNS College of Technology, Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, 13th March 2026

About Event

TEXPERIA 2026 presents the Agentic AI Ideathon, an exciting innovation challenge organized by the Department of Artificial Intelligence and Data Science at SNS College of Technology.

This event brings together creative thinkers, developers, and innovators to brainstorm and pitch impactful solutions for real-world problems using Artificial Intelligence concepts. Participants will collaborate in teams to design innovative ideas and present their solutions to an expert judging panel.

The ideathon aims to encourage creative thinking, technical innovation, and collaborative problem solving among students passionate about technology and AI.

The Ideathon is a two-round idea pitching competition where teams will propose innovative solutions to real-world challenges.

Round 1 – Idea Pitch:
Participants will be given a problem statement on the spot and must present their idea explaining the problem and their proposed AI-based solution.

Round 2 – Solution Framework:
Selected teams will further develop their idea and present a structured solution approach including workflow, feasibility, and potential implementation.

Teams will be evaluated based on innovation, feasibility, problem understanding, and clarity of presentation.

Workshops

No separate workshop sessions are included in this event. The focus is on the ideathon competition and idea pitching challenge.

Artificial Intelligence
Agentic AI Systems
Smart Automation
AI for Social Impact
AI-Driven Innovation

Event Guests

Industry experts and faculty members will be part of the judging panel for the ideathon.

NIRMAVORA FEST 2K26 is a national-level technical fest organised by the Department of Civil Engineering and Department of Mechanical Engineering in association with ICI Sustainable Engineers Club and Engineers Without Borders Club.

The fest aims to bring together innovative minds from multiple engineering disciplines to showcase creative ideas, solve real-world problems, and develop sustainable engineering solutions through collaborative competitions and technical challenges.

The fest includes two major events:

1️⃣ Designathon
A design-focused engineering challenge where participants develop innovative concepts and engineering solutions under various civil and mechanical themes.

A rapid innovation challenge where teams develop software or hardware prototypes to solve real-world engineering problems within 8 hours.

INNOVATEX - PROTOTHON 2026

INNOVATEX - PROTOTHON 2026, SNS College of Technology, Hackathon, Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, 27th February - 12th March 2026

About Event

INNOVATEX PROTOTHON – TEXPERIA’26
Organized by the Department of Electrical and Electronics Engineering
SNS College of Technology, Coimbatore

About the Event
Innovatex Protothon – TEXPERIA’26 is a dynamic innovation-driven prototyping competition designed to ignite creativity, technical excellence, and real-time problem-solving skills among aspiring engineers.

This protothon provides a platform for students to transform innovative ideas into practical working prototypes and present them before an expert panel. If you have an idea that can create real-world impact, this is your opportunity to build, innovate, and compete.

Objective
Encourage innovation and practical problem solving

Promote interdisciplinary collaboration

Develop prototyping and technical presentation skills

Bridge the gap between concepts and implementation

Provide exposure to expert evaluation

Themes
Projects can be developed under the following domains:

Renewable Energy

Aerospace

Internet of Things (IoT)

Circuit Intelligence

Open Innovations

Event Structure
Round 1 – Online PPT Screening (FREE)
Submit your idea in PPT format

Evaluation based on innovation, feasibility, impact & clarity

Shortlisted teams will move to the final round

Submission Window: 28th February – 6th March 2026

Registration link: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdyiSTC9VG2_0-J9sTS8YpsNXNDYPOuJqLwcR3INkWB2S0acA/viewform?usp=publish-editor
Round 2 – Offline Grand Finale
12th March 2026

Venue: SNSCT Campus, Coimbatore

Shortlisted teams must build and present their working prototype during the event

Team Details
Team Size: 3 – 4 Members

Open to UG students from all engineering disciplines

Interdisciplinary teams are encouraged

Registration Fee
₹400 per person (applicable for shortlisted teams participating in Round 2)

Rewards & Benefits
Prize Pool Worth ₹20,000+

Certificates for all participants

Food provided for finalists

Networking and recognition opportunities

Exposure to expert evaluation panel

Rules & Regulations
Each team must consist of 3–4 members only.

Participants must carry a valid college ID card during the offline round.

The idea submitted in Round 1 must be the same project developed for Round 2 (major deviations are not allowed without prior approval).

The prototype must be built entirely during the event. No prior prototyping, pre-assembled models, or ready-made implementations are allowed. Teams are permitted to bring raw materials and components only.

Projects must be original and innovative. Plagiarism will lead to immediate disqualification.

Teams must bring their own required components, laptops, hardware, and tools for the final round.

Power supply will be provided; any special requirements must be informed in advance.

Judges’ decisions will be final and binding.

Teams must adhere strictly to event timelines. Late submissions will not be considered.

Any form of misconduct, misbehavior, or damage to property will result in disqualification.

The organizing committee reserves the right to modify rules if necessary.

Evaluation Criteria
Innovation & Creativity

Technical Feasibility

Working Prototype & Implementation

Real-world Impact

Presentation & Clarity

Web agent Hackathon 2026, Coimbatore Institute of Technology, Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, 6th - 7th March 2026

About Event

Intellina 2026 – Web Agent Hackathon is a flagship technical event organized by the Department of Artificial Intelligence and Data  Science at Coimbatore Institute of Technology. Designed to bring together innovative minds from across institutions, this hackathon challenges participants to build intelligent web agents capable of transforming the way humans interact with digital systems.

The event focuses on cutting-edge domains such as Intelligent Form Filling Agents, Autonomous Web Navigation Agents, Personal Productivity Automation Agents, and Real-Time Monitoring and Action Agents. Participants will design and develop smart, AI-powered solutions that can automate tasks, enhance productivity, and create seamless digital experiences.

Intellina 2026 follows a two-round format. Round 1 consists of abstract screening, where teams present their innovative ideas and proposed solutions. Shortlisted teams will advance to Round 2, an intensive 8-hour hackathon sprint where they will build and demonstrate their working prototypes. The competition encourages creativity, technical excellence, and practical implementation of AI-driven systems.

With a prize pool of ₹25,000 and e-certificates for all participants, the event aims to foster collaboration, innovation, and real-world problem-solving skills among students. Teams of 1–4 members can participate, making it an excellent platform for aspiring developers, AI enthusiasts, and problem-solvers to showcase their talents.Geographic Reference

SELECT Makeathon '26, Vellore Institute of Technology, Hackathon, Vellore, Tamil Nadu, 26th - 27th March 2026

About Event

The SELECT Makeathon is a flagship 36-hour intensive innovation challenge organized by the School of Electrical and Engineering (SELECT) at VIT Vellore. The event brings together students, researchers, and professionals to develop functional prototypes—spanning both hardware and software—to address real-world industry problem statements. Participants benefit from expert mentorship, technical guidance, and the opportunity to showcase their solutions to a panel of over 30 industrial judges.

(TRACKS)
Motion Matrix: Rethinking movement and rebuilding mobility.
Planet Earth: Focusing on agriculture and sustainability.
Iron Dome: Defense and strategic security systems.

Texperia 2026 is an inter-college technical fest organized by the Department of Mechanical and Mechatronics Engineering (Additive Manufacturing). Open to students from all colleges, Texperia 2026 provides a platform to innovate, compete, and transform concepts into real-world impact.

About Event

Step into the future with ZenAI Hackathon - a high-energy, 24-hour offline AI innovation challenge crafted to test your creativity, technical skills, and problem-solving abilities.

This inter-college hackathon brings together young innovators to collaborate, build, and deploy impactful AI-driven solutions within just 24 hours. From ideation to implementation, teams will transform ideas into working prototypes under pressure - showcasing endurance, innovation, and teamwork.

If you're passionate about Artificial Intelligence and ready to build something extraordinary, this is your stage.

Participants will work on real-world AI problem statements revealed on the day of the hackathon.

Open to undergraduate students only

3-5 problem statement focused on AI, ML, and Data  Science domains

iStart Hackathon 2026

iStart Hackathon 2026, Knowledge Institute of Technology, Salem, Tamil Nadu, 27th - 28th March 2026

About Event

iStart Hackathon’26 is a 24-hour non-stop innovation challenge organized by iStartKIOT MX Incubator Foundation, Knowledge Institute of Technology, Salem. The event aims to bring together college students, student startups, and tech enthusiasts from various institutions to ideate, build, and present impactful solutions to real-world problems.

The hackathon focuses on fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and startup development under the vision of Innovate India 2047. Participants will submit their ideas in Stage 1 (online screening), and shortlisted teams will compete in a 24-hour offline hackathon to develop working prototypes and business models.

Selected teams will receive cash prizes, mentorship, incubation support, and potential seed funding opportunities. The event serves as a launchpad for transforming innovative ideas into scalable startups.
Stage 1: Idea Submission & Screening (Online Mode)

1. Event Announcement & Registrations Open
📅 From: 01 March 2026
Participants from various colleges register and submit their innovative ideas through the official registration portal.

2. Last Date for Idea Submission
📅 12 March 2026
Teams must submit their problem statement, proposed solution, innovation impact, and basic implementation plan before the deadline.

3. Screening & Shortlisting Process
📅 13 March 2026 – 18 March 2026
All submitted ideas will be evaluated by an expert panel based on:

Innovation & Creativity

Feasibility

Technical Implementation Plan

Scalability & Startup Potential

Social / Industrial Impact

4. Shortlisted Teams Announcement
📅 20 March 2026
Selected teams will be invited to participate in the 24-hour offline hackathon (Stage 2).

🔹 Stage 2: 24-Hour Non-Stop Hackathon (Offline – KIOT Campus, Salem)

5. Reporting & Registration Confirmation
📅 27 March 2026 – Morning
Teams complete verification and onboarding formalities.

6. Hackathon Inauguration & Problem Briefing
📅 27 March 2026 – Forenoon
Official inauguration and final instructions by organizers and mentors.

7. 24-Hour Development Sprint Begins
📅 27 March 2026 (Start)
Teams begin prototype development, coding, hardware building, model designing, and business validation.

Mentors will provide:

Technical Guidance

Business Model Support

Startup & Pitch Assistance

8. 24-Hour Development Sprint Ends
📅 28 March 2026

9. Final Demonstration & Jury Evaluation
📅 28 March 2026
Teams present:

Working Prototype / Model

Technical Architecture

Business Plan

Market Strategy

Impact & Scalability

10. Valedictory & Prize Distribution
📅 28 March 2026
Winners announced.
Top teams receive:

₹25,000 Cash Prize Pool

Mentorship

Incubation Support

Protospark 2026

Protospark 2026, Velammal Institute of Technology, Technical Innovation Fest, Chennai, Tamil Nadu, 26th - 27th June 2026

About Event

Protospark 2026 is a premier technical innovation fest organized by Velammal Institute of Technology, Chennai, with the vision of fostering creativity, research orientation, and industry-driven problem solving among engineering students. Centered around the theme “Designing the Ideas That Shape Tomorrow,” the event serves as a dynamic platform for students to conceptualize, prototype, and present innovative solutions to real-world challenges.

The fest brings together young innovators from diverse engineering disciplines to collaborate, compete, and showcase projects aligned with emerging industry trends and societal needs. Protospark focuses on transforming theoretical knowledge into practical applications through working models, prototypes, and technology-driven solutions.

The event covers a wide spectrum of domains including Artificial Intelligence, Robotics and Industry 4.0, Information and Communication Technology, Clean and Renewable Energy, Smart Transportation, Healthcare Technologies, Agriculture and Food Technology, Waste Management, Climate Change and Disaster Management, Construction Technology, and Smart  Education.

Over two days, participants will engage in project demonstrations, technical evaluations, and expert interactions with academicians and industry professionals. Projects will be assessed based on innovation, technical feasibility, scalability, sustainability, and real-world impact.

Protospark 2026 aims to:

Encourage innovation and entrepreneurial thinking among students

Strengthen industry–academia collaboration

Promote sustainable and socially responsible technological solutions

Provide exposure to emerging technologies and research opportunities

Identify and reward outstanding engineering talent

By creating a competitive yet collaborative environment, Protospark empowers students to move beyond classroom learning and contribute meaningfully to technological advancement and societal development.Educational Institution Directory

Protospark 2026 features a series of innovation-driven technical events designed to showcase engineering talent and practical problem-solving skills. Key events include:

About Event

KALAM 2K26

KALAM 2K26 is a flagship technical fest organized by the Department of  Computer  Science and Engineering (Cyber Security) at Sri Shakthi Institute of Engineering and Technology. The fest serves as a dynamic platform for students to explore emerging technologies, enhance problem-solving skills, and transform innovative ideas into impactful digital solutions.

The fest promotes open innovation across various domains of Computer Science including Artificial Intelligence, Data Science, Cybersecurity, Software Development, Cloud Computing, and other emerging technologies. It aims to bridge academia and industry by encouraging collaboration, creativity, and real-world application of technical knowledge.

Through competitive events, industry interaction, and hands-on technical challenges, KALAM 2K26 empowers students to build, innovate, and lead in the evolving digital era.Mechanical Engineering tools

About Event

The Inter-College 24-Hour Hackathon is a dynamic platform designed to bring together young innovators, developers, and problem-solvers from various institutions. This event challenges participants to transform creative ideas into functional prototypes within a limited time frame of 24 hours.

The Hackathon fosters collaboration, innovation, and real-time problem-solving by encouraging students to address contemporary challenges using emerging technologies. It provides an opportunity to enhance technical skills, think critically, and work as a team in a competitive yet supportive environment.
